---
title: "Polygonal vs Non-Polygonal and KQ vs QQ quality views"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(purrr)
```

Importing the Data

```{r, echo=FALSE}
file_path <- "data/bullets_2024.xlsx"

kq_data <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "KQ responses (n=1575)")
qq_data <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "QQ responses (n=1581)")
cset_data <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "Cset (n=1240)")
```

Data Cleaning

```{r, echo=FALSE}
phase_label <- "Primary (Baseline)"

kq <- kq_data %>%
  filter(Phase == phase_label) %>%
  mutate(
    Difficulty = if_else(K01_Q1value == "NoValue", "NoValue", K09_Difficulty),
    Comparison = "KQ"
  ) %>%
  select(AnonID, Cset, Mating, Difficulty, Comparison)

qq <- qq_data %>%
  filter(Phase == phase_label) %>%
  mutate(
    Difficulty = if_else(Q01_Q1value == "NoValue" | Q02_Q2value == "NoValue", "NoValue", Q09_Difficulty),
    Comparison = "QQ"
  ) %>%
  select(AnonID, Cset, Mating, Difficulty, Comparison)

cset <- cset_data %>%select( Cset, Type,`Cset Category (Detailed)`, `Cset Quality (voted)` ) %>%
  rename(Category = `Cset Category (Detailed)`, CsetQuality = `Cset Quality (voted)` )

responses <- bind_rows(kq, qq) %>%
  left_join(cset, by = "Cset") %>%
  mutate(Category = str_squish(Category), Difficulty = factor(Difficulty, levels = c("VEasy", "Easy", "Norm", "NoValue", "Diff", "VDiff")))
```

Data Cleaning for Item Sets with at least one No Value response

```{r, echo=FALSE}
# KQ: restrict to Primary (Baseline) then filter Csets where any examiner has NoValue
kq_primary <- kq_data %>%
  filter(Phase == "Primary (Baseline)")

kq_items_with_NoValue <- kq_primary %>%
  group_by(Cset) %>%
  filter(any(K08_Conclusion == "NoValue")) %>%
  ungroup()


# QQ: same idea
qq_primary <- qq_data %>%
  filter(Phase == "Primary (Baseline)")

qq_items_with_NoValue <- qq_primary %>%
  group_by(Cset) %>%
  filter(any(Q08_Conclusion == "NoValue")) %>%
  ungroup()
```


KQ vs QQ decision rates by quality (heatmap)

```{r, echo=FALSE}
quality_data <- responses %>%
  filter(!str_detect(Category, "Polygonal"), !str_detect(Category, "FMJ-JHP|JHP-FMJ"), !str_detect(Category, "Diff Caliber") ) %>% filter(!is.na(CsetQuality))

plot_quality <- quality_data %>%
  count(Mating, Comparison, CsetQuality, Difficulty, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(Mating, Comparison, CsetQuality) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(plot_quality, aes(x = CsetQuality, y = Difficulty, fill = pct)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = percent(pct, accuracy = 0.1)), size = 2.6) +
  facet_grid(Mating ~ Comparison) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "darkblue", labels = percent) +
  labs( title = "Difficulty Decision Distribution by Quality (KQ vs QQ)",
    x = "Quality",
    y = NULL, fill = "% of responses"
  ) 
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
## starting from your script, after plot_quality is created
## plot_quality has: Mating, Comparison (KQ/QQ), CsetQuality (A:A, A:B, A:C,...),
## Difficulty (VEasy,...), pct (proportion of responses)  [file:3]

# keep only qualities you want in the toy bank
toy_qualities <- c("A:A", "A:B", "A:C")

diff_probs <- plot_quality %>%
  filter(CsetQuality %in% toy_qualities) %>%
  select(Mating, Comparison, CsetQuality, Difficulty, pct) %>%
  group_by(Mating, Comparison, CsetQuality) %>%
  mutate(p = pct / sum(pct)) %>%           # re‑normalise, just in case
  ungroup()

# helper: simulate one scenario -----------------------------------------
simulate_scenario <- function(
  n_items      = 10000,
  bank_mix_KQ  = c("A:A" = 0.5, "A:B" = 0.3, "A:C" = 0.2),
  bank_mix_QQ  = c("A:A" = 0.5, "A:B" = 0.3, "A:C" = 0.2)
) {
  bank_mix <- bind_rows(
    tibble(Comparison = "KQ",
           CsetQuality = names(bank_mix_KQ),
           bank_p = as.numeric(bank_mix_KQ)),
    tibble(Comparison = "QQ",
           CsetQuality = names(bank_mix_QQ),
           bank_p = as.numeric(bank_mix_QQ))
  )

  # expand to per‑item draws
  draws <- tibble(item = 1:n_items) %>%
    crossing(Mating = c("Mated","Nonmated"),
             Comparison = c("KQ","QQ")) %>%
    group_by(Mating, Comparison) %>%
    mutate(
      # sample item quality for each item from the chosen bank mix
      CsetQuality = sample(
        bank_mix$CsetQuality[bank_mix$Comparison == unique(Comparison)],
        size = n(),
        replace = TRUE,
        prob = bank_mix$bank_p[bank_mix$Comparison == unique(Comparison)]
      )
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    left_join(diff_probs,
              by = c("Mating","Comparison","CsetQuality")) %>%
    group_by(item, Mating, Comparison, CsetQuality) %>%
    summarise(
      Difficulty = sample(Difficulty, size = 1, prob = p),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # summarise simulated difficulty distribution
  draws %>%
    count(Mating, Comparison, Difficulty, name = "n") %>%
    group_by(Mating, Comparison) %>%
    mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
    ungroup()
}

# example: baseline bank composition (same KQ vs QQ)
sim_baseline <- simulate_scenario(
  n_items = 20000,
  bank_mix_KQ = c("A:A" = 0.6, "A:B" = 0.3, "A:C" = 0.1),
  bank_mix_QQ = c("A:A" = 0.6, "A:B" = 0.3, "A:C" = 0.1)
)

# example: QQ has more low quality (A:C) than KQ
sim_shifted <- simulate_scenario(
  n_items = 20000,
  bank_mix_KQ = c("A:A" = 0.6, "A:B" = 0.3, "A:C" = 0.1),
  bank_mix_QQ = c("A:A" = 0.3, "A:B" = 0.3, "A:C" = 0.4)
)

# quick comparison table of overall difficulty rates
sim_compare <- bind_rows(
  sim_baseline %>% mutate(scenario = "baseline"),
  sim_shifted  %>% mutate(scenario = "QQ_more_AC")
)

sim_compare

```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
ggplot(sim_compare,
       aes(x = Difficulty, y = pct, fill = scenario)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_grid(Mating ~ Comparison) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(y = "Simulated % of decisions",
       title = "Effect of item bank quality mix on difficulty decisions")

```
